title: Routine Monthly Invoice — Known Vendor, Matching Details
benchmark_purpose: Baseline sanity check. A genuinely clean invoice with no anomalies. Tests that the system does not over-escalate when all signals are green. If a system escalates this, its false-positive rate is too high for production.
naive_system_likely_conclusion: ALLOW — everything matches. No system should struggle with this.

email_thread.subject: Invoice AOC-2026-0315 — March Cleaning Services
email_thread.body:
Hi AP Team,

Please find attached our invoice for March cleaning services per PO-TC-7700.

Invoice: AOC-2026-0315
Amount: $8,400.00
Terms: Net-30
Due: April 15, 2026

Please remit to the bank account on file.

Thank you,
Linda Marsh
Billing
Apex Office Cleaning
(555) 190-3300

attachments.content_summary: Invoice from Apex Office Cleaning. AOC-2026-0315. PO: PO-TC-7700. Office cleaning — main building ($5,600), annex building ($1,800), deep-clean restrooms ($1,000). Total: $8,400.00. Net-30. Bank: account on file (****2211).

org_policies.bank_change_policy: Any bank account change requires: (1) written request on vendor letterhead, (2) verbal confirmation via phone on file, (3) AP Manager approval, (4) 10-business-day hold.

payment_approval_thresholds.over_100000: AP Clerk + AP Manager + Controller + CFO

org_policies.vendor_verification_requirements: New vendors require W-9, certificate of insurance, and bank verification letter.

hidden_ground_truth.explanation: Completely routine monthly invoice from a nine-year vendor. Bank matches. Domain matches. Email auth passes. Amount is within historical range ($7,800–$9,200). PO is valid. Approval chain is complete for the $8,400 amount (under $25K threshold requires only AP Clerk + AP Manager). No anomalies of any kind.

evidence_signals.signal: Sender domain apexcleaning.com matches vendor record
Approval chain complete for sub-$25K amount

scoring_targets.false_positive_risk: Very low — no plausible reason to escalate. Escalating this scenario indicates a miscalibrated system.
scoring_targets.architecture_differentiation_notes: Baseline coverage. All architectures should ALLOW. Failure here indicates systemic over-escalation or a broken signal parser. Use to calibrate precision.

notes_for_humans: This is the floor. Any system that escalates this has a fundamental false-positive problem. Include in Tier 3 as a sanity baseline — if a system fails this, the remaining results are not meaningful.
